emails.subject: You Won the TikTok Creator Raffle!
emails.body:
Hey there superstar!
You've won a free phone stand and TikTok hoodie for your awesome videos.
Click here to enter your address and claim it!
emails.explanation: It looks fun and legit, but TikTok doesn't run raffles like this — and they wouldn't ask for your address via random email links.

emails.subject: You're really cute... 😊
emails.body:
Hey... I found your email somewhere very secret.
You seem pretty cool. Let's chat.
Click here to see my pictures.
Also email me back if you want to talk cutie. 💌
emails.explanation: This is a serious red flag. Never respond to strangers — especially adults sending you links or calling you 'cute.'

emails.subject: Cyber Safety Poster Contest 🖍️
emails.body:
Hello students!
Submit your entry for the annual Cyber Safety Poster Contest by this Friday.
Winners get a pizza party! 🍕
emails.explanation: This is from a teacher at school, with a normal request and no suspicious links.

emails.subject: New Cookie Characters + Cherry Blossom Decor 🌸
emails.body:
Hi sweet friend!
Our spring update is here!
Log in to meet Cherry Blossom Cookie and decorate your village.

Read more here.
emails.explanation: Real update news with no strange links, sent from a believable game email domain.

emails.subject: You've earned 5,000 Nook Miles!
emails.body:
Hey Islander!
Tom Nook says you've earned bonus miles for being awesome! 🥝
Click here to claim them now.
emails.explanation: The email pretends to be from Nintendo but uses a suspicious domain. It's a sneaky phishing attempt.
